subtitle: Case 69001655 catch-up at 10am, urgent reference request, Cority import issue
context: Nathan Kirwan is out of office at the People Forum until at least 26 June 2026 with limited email access, blocking responses on Staff EES Information for HESA - Research Data Items (REF). The Cority Employee Import process for OxfordUniversity encountered an SFTP connection error on 22 June 2026 at 17:15, with the Cority Hosted Integration (CHI) Team notified, requiring your follow-up to confirm resolution status. Case 69001655 with Access Group has been escalating since 19 June and you have a catch-up with Maura McGlynn today at 10:00 on Microsoft Teams to review progress, but Maura's update from 24 June remains unread and may contain critical information for that meeting. An urgent reference request for Nikolas Sharkey from TSS Enquiries (received 23 June at 12:11) has waited 48+ hours without your response and requires immediate action. University of Oxford case FP 68261303 regarding Multi Company Setup is pending with Simon Burford's proposal from 23 June requiring your review with attachments. Open first: the unread email from Maura McGlynn about Case 69001655 Catch-Up to prepare for your 10:00 Teams meeting today, then respond to the TSS Enquiries reference request before end of business Thursday 25 June.

prioritiesToday.text: Web pages and URL linking -- job posting updates
prioritiesToday.description: Internal Mobility Principles page moved to a new OxIntranet URL. Three pages needed link updates: main ops intranet page, professional services search page, and individual vacancy application pages. New URL confirmed live on 3 June. Old page retired 23/24 June. E-Form save bug in Search Appointments V4 was blocking updates -- Maura McGlynn (AG) applied a config fix on 23 June.
actions: [03 Jun 2026] Meeting with Laura Porter -- reviewed three pages needing link updates.

[23 Jun 2026] Urgent escalation -- unable to save changes in Search Appointments V4 E-Form Maintenance (case 69001555). Maura McGlynn applied a config fix -- reports changes now saving. Kevin asked to review and confirm.
[AWAITING] Kevin to review Maura's config fix and confirm E-Form save issue resolved in Search Appointments V4.
[TODO] Complete remaining URL updates on vacancy application pages once E-Form save confirmed working.

prioritiesToday.description: Pay Code 121 (report reference PAYPAY25) needs to be added to the SQL in HR Reporting. Raised by Michael in 1-1 on 15 June -- needed for this month's payroll run. Process: identify which .RDL report(s) handle pay code filtering, edit SQL, render in Visual Studio, deploy DEV -> TEST -> QA -> LIVE.
actions: [15 Jun 2026] Action raised by Michael in 1-1 -- Pay Code 121 / PAYPAY25 missing from SQL report suite. Needed for this month's payroll.
[TODO] Confirm with Michael which specific report(s) in the suite need amending.

[TODO] Render updated report(s) in Visual Studio and deploy DEV -> TEST -> QA -> LIVE.
[TODO] Verify output in LIVE environment before payroll run.

prioritiesWeek.description: Smart notes in Cority CoreEHS+ (GX2) do not allow edits after save and close. Raised November 2025, dev ref CCC-19664. Cority's official position (confirmed 08 Jun 2026 by Mehran Riaz, support leadership): this is an INTENDED feature -- not a bug or missing feature. Cority classify the request to allow post-save edits as an enhancement, and it is NOT on their current roadmap. No timeline will be provided. Beta/early access ('enhanced access support') requires Premium Support, which Oxford does not have. Oxford has no named account manager -- we are in a pool.
actions: [Nov 2025] Ticket 00476764 raised by James -- smart notes cannot be edited after save/close.

[08 Jun 2026] Mehran Riaz (support leadership) responded: feature is INTENDED behaviour, classified as enhancement, NOT on roadmap. No timeline. Premium Support required for beta access (Oxford does not have it). No named account manager -- pool model.
[16 Jun 2026] Raised at Cority PUG with Christopher Sanders. PUG acknowledged smart notes as outstanding; Kevin to escalate directly with Cority for a firm delivery date.
[TODO] Chase Cority for firm delivery date on CCC-19664 draft/save functionality.
[TODO] Decide whether to pursue via sales/account route or accept as a known limitation and communicate to stakeholders.

prioritiesWeek.description: The shared drive was moved to SharePoint but guidance documentation was not updated to reflect the new location. Only Kevin and Lee were managing the migration, so other team members did not know where to find documents. Simon has raised this and Kevin needs to respond in writing before recommending an alignment meeting.
actions: [08 Jun] Issue identified and raised by Simon -- shared drive moved to SharePoint, guidance not updated.
[09 Jun 2026] Root cause confirmed: documents exist and are correctly placed; signposting is the issue.
[TODO] Write response to Simon explaining the situation and confirming documents are accessible.
[TODO] Recommend alignment meeting to get whole team up to speed on SharePoint structure and org hierarchy documentation.
[TODO] Touch base with Michael separately on this.

prioritiesWeek.text: REF attributes via ESS -- end of July deadline (Sarah Rowles)
prioritiesWeek.description: Sarah Rowles has added a new item to the HR Systems Roadmap: sharing specific REF attributes with staff via Employee Self-Service (ESS) by end of July 2026, to enable the REF appeals process. Simon Burford and Kevin Lelitte are named. Nathan (Sarah's team) is looking at it from the technical side. Anne is sharing last time's guidance, but Sarah has flagged this is NOT a like-for-like repeat. Key question in thread: will this require a new UDF to be set up and data loaded, or re-use an existing UDF? Athena Artuso noted that UDFs were set up in advance for future REF cycles, so that stage may be future-proof -- but it depends on what the specific REF attributes are. Sarah has explicitly flagged that the team is thin on the ground in July and this will need FA input, testing time, and business change support for user instructions.
actions: [24 Jun 2026] Sarah Rowles flagged new Roadmap item -- REF attributes via ESS, end of July deadline, for REF appeals process.
[24 Jun 2026] Thread discussion: UDF question raised -- Athena believes UDFs set up in advance but depends on specific attributes. Likely just a data load if correct.
[TODO] Establish with Nathan (Sarah's team) exactly which REF attributes need to be surfaced via ESS.
[TODO] Confirm whether existing UDFs cover the required attributes or new setup needed.
[TODO] Assess FA input and testing resource required -- flag capacity risk given July constraints.
[TODO] Raise at Managers Meeting to agree resourcing and timeline.
